message:
fix(retry): align is_retryable with error_kind for PaneOperation::PaneNotFound

[HIGH] retry.rs:425-431 previously blanket-approved every PaneOperation
variant as retryable, directly contradicting error_kind() in error.rs
which classifies PaneOperation { PaneNotFound } as NetworkErrorKind::
Permanent. The with_smart_retry harness consults is_retryable to decide
whether to re-enter the loop; under the old default it would spin on
a pane that literally does not exist, burning the caller's retry budget
for no possible outcome.

87d4546b (ft-h9g0q) flagged this as deferred follow-up work:
"A follow-up can inspect `source` on each variant and flip non-
transient failures (e.g. PermissionDenied) to false." This is that
follow-up for the PaneNotFound case — identified during a review pass
over the last-20-commits swarm diff in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Adds 4 unit tests covering the three new variants' retry classification:
- not_retryable_pane_operation_pane_not_found
- is_retryable_pane_operation_backend
- is_retryable_runtime_operation_watch_closed
- is_retryable_watchdog_warning_read

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
